author: Edgar Allan Poe

claims.narrative_anchor: police description of exhaustive hidden-place searches
claims.notes: This is a useful control because the police premise is described before Dupin's decisive observation is narrated.
claims.speaker: police search premise
claims.text: The stolen letter must be hidden in a secret compartment or concealed recess.

claims.notes: Corrective claim appears as part of the explanation rather than as reader-available decisive evidence.
claims.speaker: Dupin
claims.text: The letter is disguised and left in plain sight.

decisive_evidence.narrative_anchor: Dupin reports his prior visit and observation after the fact
decisive_evidence.notes: The key observation is attributed retrospectively to Dupin, making this a control for withheld decisive evidence.
decisive_evidence.text: Dupin personally observes the disguised letter in plain sight before reporting the solution.

metadata.source_collection: The Gift: A Christmas and New Year's Present for 1845

notes: Candidate control annotation for withheld decisive evidence; useful for distinguishing fair-play recognition from retrospective explanation.
title: The Purloined Letter
